seoTitle: New Chum vs New Farm — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between New Chum and New Farm. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: New Chum vs New Farm: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Chum and New Farm.
heading: New Chum vs New Farm — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Chum, Queensland and New Farm, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Farm has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to New Chum (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Chum or New Farm safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Chum has a flood risk score of 50/100, while New Farm scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Chum scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. New Farm scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Chum or New Farm?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Farm has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
